title: Discrete-time survival head for pit hazard, then read off next-lap probability
summary: Discrete-time survival converts time-to-event prediction into conditional hazard classification on person-period rows.
rationale: Discrete-time survival converts time-to-event prediction into conditional hazard classification on person-period rows. Pit timing is naturally a time-to-event problem, and survival-consistent hazards can use richer supervision than a single next-lap binary while still outputting `P(pit on next lap)`.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race stint, expand training targets to remaining laps until next pit/end-of-race; fit a compact model for conditional hazard by lap-to-go or tyre-age bucket with shared covariates, then use the t+1 hazard as submission probability or blend it with the current binary classifier.
expected_effect: Should improve signal extraction from long stints and reduce brittle one-lap thresholding, especially in late-stint regions where hazard ramps sharply.
risk: Person-period expansion can explode row count and overfit if baseline hazard structure is too flexible.
